title: Process Control & Instrumentation
outline: This course aims to present a unified and holistic view of industrial control, automation and instrumentation. It covers topics on industrial automation, measurements and instrumentation, and introduces advanced control methods. Specific topics are: the in dustrial automation hierarchy; automation drawings – P&ID diagrams; Programmable Logic Controllers; Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition; Distributed Control Systems; Sensing and Measurement Techniques; Batch Processes; Automation Networks; Safety Systems; and a selection of Advanced Control topics such as Nonlinear, Quantitative Feedback Theory, H -infinity and Model Predictive Control. The course aims to develop knowledge, skills and values through a balanced integration of lecturers, tutorials, laboratory and project work.
